title: Adopt a Retail Mac into ABM (Apple Configurator)

steps.title: Check the Mac qualifies
steps.instructions: Apple silicon or a T2 chip, macOS 12.0.1 or later. Older Intel Macs without T2 cannot be adopted - keep them on /mdm Jamf manual.
steps.done_when: Model confirmed eligible.

steps.title: Prepare the iPhone
steps.instructions: Install Apple Configurator from the App Store. Sign in with the Managed Apple ID.
steps.done_when: Configurator open and signed in.

steps.title: Preset auto-assignment in ABM (one time)
steps.instructions: ABM > Settings > Device Management Settings > Devices added by Apple Configurator > assign to the Jamf server. With this set, adopted Macs skip manual assignment.

steps.title: Erase the Mac to Setup Assistant
steps.instructions: New in box: just power on. Used: System Settings > General > Transfer or Reset > Erase All Content and Settings. Join Wi-Fi during setup.
steps.done_when: Mac sits at the country/region pane.

steps.title: Pair with the iPhone
steps.instructions: Hold the iPhone near the Mac at the country/region pane. The Mac shows a pairing animation; scan it with the iPhone camera in Configurator.
steps.done_when: Device visible in ABM > Devices, assigned to Jamf.

steps.title: Restart Setup Assistant and enroll
steps.instructions: Erase again if setup went past the region pane. This time the Remote Management screen appears and the Mac enrolls into Jamf. From now on this Mac uses /mdm Jamf auto in the Workstation SOP.
steps.done_when: Mac shows in Jamf as managed.

meta.why: Retail-bought Macs have no Apple Business Manager record, so zero-touch enrollment (ADE) can never happen. Adoption gives the Mac an ABM record - after this it enrolls like a business-bought machine, on every future rebuild.
meta.how: Pair the erased Mac with an iPhone running Apple Configurator at Setup Assistant; ABM records the device; Jamf takes it from there.
meta.tools:
iPhone (iOS 15+) with the Apple Configurator app
iPhone signed in with a Managed Apple ID - Administrator or Device Enrollment Manager role
Wi-Fi both devices can reach
meta.safety:
The Mac must be erased - adoption only works from Setup Assistant. Back up anything on it first.
30-day provisional period: the device can be released from ABM during it. Keep the Mac in IT hands until enrollment is confirmed.

steps.title: Internet handoff
steps.instructions: Who terminates the circuit, where the demarc lives, what the gateway is.
steps.done_when: Modem/ONT model, public IP or PPPoE noted.
steps.record: Gateway device as an asset; WAN details in its notes.

steps.title: Core switching & routing
steps.why: A topology nobody can follow at 2am is decoration.
steps.instructions: Every switch and router: name, location, uplinks. @-mention the devices so this doc stays wired to inventory.
steps.done_when: Every port that matters is accounted for.

steps.title: Wireless
steps.instructions: SSIDs, which VLAN each lands on, where the controller lives.
steps.done_when: All SSIDs listed with their purpose.

steps.title: VLANs & subnets
steps.instructions: The table: VLAN id, name, subnet, gateway, DHCP source.
steps.done_when: Every subnet in use appears here.

steps.title: Diagram
steps.instructions: Paste the topology diagram right here - screenshots land inline.
steps.done_when: Diagram reflects current state.

steps.title: What gets backed up
steps.why: An unlisted system is an unprotected system.
steps.instructions: The complete list: servers, shares, databases, mailboxes, endpoints - and explicitly what does NOT.
steps.done_when: Nothing in production is unaccounted for.

steps.title: Where backups go
steps.instructions: Targets and tiers: local NAS, offsite, cloud. Retention per tier. 3-2-1 or the documented reason why not.
steps.done_when: Every tier has a named destination and retention.

steps.title: Schedule & monitoring
steps.why: A backup that fails silently is a rumor.
steps.instructions: When each job runs, where failures alert, who sees the alert.
steps.done_when: A failed job pages a human within 24h.

steps.title: Verification cadence
steps.instructions: How often test-restores happen and who does them.
steps.done_when: Last verification date recorded below.

steps.title: Declare and scope
steps.why: Restores done in panic skip steps.
steps.instructions: What is lost? File, server, site? Who declares an incident?
steps.done_when: Scope written down before touching anything.

steps.title: Locate the recovery point
steps.instructions: Which tier holds the newest good copy; how far back is acceptable.
steps.done_when: RPO decision recorded.

steps.title: Restore
steps.instructions: The actual commands/console steps per system - fill these in per client.
steps.done_when: Data back, integrity spot-checked.
steps.record: What was restored, from which point, by whom.

steps.title: Post-restore
steps.instructions: Verify services, tell the affected humans, write down what broke the original.
steps.done_when: Users confirm; root cause noted in an incident page.

steps.title: Who to call at 2am
steps.instructions: Primary and backup humans, with real phone numbers. Client-side contact too.
steps.done_when: Two humans reachable for every system.

steps.title: Where access lives
steps.why: This doc points AT credentials; it never contains them.
steps.instructions: Which registry accounts open what - @-mention the break-glass accounts; passwords stay in the registry, never here.
steps.done_when: Every critical system maps to a registry account.

steps.title: Physical access
steps.instructions: Building, server room, alarm codes owner - who holds keys.

steps.title: Escalation paths
steps.instructions: Vendor support lines and contract numbers for when it is beyond us.
steps.done_when: Every vendor with a support contract listed.

steps.title: Circuits
steps.why: The outage call needs the circuit ID, not a guess.
steps.instructions: Provider, circuit ID, service address, bandwidth, static IPs - per site.
steps.done_when: Every site has its circuit documented.

steps.title: Support & SLA
steps.instructions: Provider support number, account number, SLA terms, escalation contact.

steps.title: Failover
steps.instructions: What happens when the primary dies - LTE backup, second circuit, or documented nothing.
steps.done_when: Failover tested or honestly marked untested.
